channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NYC's Mamdani condemns Tuberville's anti-Muslim posts as "bigotry"
item.description: Speaking at a public iftar dinner, held to break the daily Ramadan fast, New York City Mayor Mamdani described Sen. Tuberville's anti-Muslim rhetoric as "bigotry" and "hatred."
item.content:encoded:
Speaking at a public iftar dinner, held to break the daily Ramadan fast, New York City Mayor Mamdani described Sen. Tuberville's anti-Muslim rhetoric as "bigotry" and "hatred."

(Image credit: Andres Kudacki)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: NASA targets Artemis II crewed moon mission for April 1 launch
item.description: A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.
item.content:encoded:
A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: What we know about the attack on a synagogue in Michigan
item.description: A man drove a truck into a synagogue in Michigan, according to authorities. The suspected attacker is dead and a guard was injured.
item.content:encoded: A man drove a truck into a synagogue in Michigan, according to authorities. The suspected attacker is dead and a guard was injured.
item.dc:creator: Alex McLenon

item.title: Paying families of organ donors would save lives, these economists say
item.description: Thousands die every year waiting for an organ donation. The Indicator team talks to two economists who argue that paying the families of organ donors would save lives.
item.content:encoded: Thousands die every year waiting for an organ donation. The Indicator team talks to two economists who argue that paying the families of organ donors would save lives.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: FBI says its taking over the Michigan synagogue attack investigation
item.description: Security officers at Temple Israel "engaged with the suspect" after a vehicle rammed into the building, according to Oakland County Sheriff Mike Bouchard.
item.content:encoded:
Security officers at Temple Israel "engaged with the suspect" after a vehicle rammed into the building, according to Oakland County Sheriff Mike Bouchard.

(Image credit: Emily Elconin)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.content:encoded: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman

item.title: 'Songs from the Hole': The story behind JJ'88's documentary and visual album
item.description: The visual album and documentary Songs from the Hole tells the story of James Jacobs, the hip-hop artist JJ'88, as he reflects on his coming-of-age within California's state prison system.
item.content:encoded:
Songs from the Hole. Before the song starts, protagonist and producer James "JJ'88" Jacobs describes meditating on his and others' redemption while incarcerated and in solitary confinement.'/>

The visual album and documentary Songs from the Hole tells the story of James Jacobs, the hip-hop artist JJ'88, as he reflects on his coming-of-age within California's state prison system.

(Image credit: Courtesy of Netflix)
item.dc:creator: Kara Frame

item.title: The Trump administration's crackdown on immigrant truckers shifts into higher gear
item.description: The White House wants tougher rules for commercial licenses after several high-profile crashes involving foreign-born drivers. But critics say that would do little to make the nation's roads safer.
item.content:encoded:
The White House wants tougher rules for commercial licenses after several high-profile crashes involving foreign-born drivers. But critics say that would do little to make the nation's roads safer.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: DOJ's program to restore gun rights to felons raises questions about transparency
item.description: The Department of Justice has quietly restarted a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One name on the list is raising questions about transparency.
item.content:encoded: The Department of Justice has quietly restarted a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One name on the list is raising questions about transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Program trains volunteers to be a listening ear and provide a 'human connection'
item.description: Friendship Bench DC, an adaptation of Friendship Bench Zimbabwe, trains older volunteers to sit and listen to people who need someone to talk to for free.
item.content:encoded: Friendship Bench DC, an adaptation of Friendship Bench Zimbabwe, trains older volunteers to sit and listen to people who need someone to talk to for free.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
